summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the UDM server sends an HTTP POST request to an attacker-controlled DeregCallbackUri when a re-registration with a different GUAMI occurs. The mock infrastructure (NRF, UDR) enables the full registration flow, and the exploit triggers the SSRF by first registering with a malicious callback URL then re-registering with a different GUAMI.
expected_security_impact: The UDM server should make an outbound HTTP POST request to the attacker-controlled URL (http://ssrf-listener:9999/ssrf-callback) stored as DeregCallbackUri during the first AMF registration. This request should arrive at the listener, proving the server-side request forgery.
actual_observed_result: Running the reproduction pipeline caused the UDM to send an HTTP POST to the attacker-controlled callback URL. The listener received the deregistration notification request containing the UdmUecmDeregistrationData payload, confirming the SSRF.
verification_method: A Python HTTP listener on port 9999 captures all incoming requests. The exploit script checks whether the listener received a POST request to /ssrf-callback after the second AMF registration. The verification distinguishes the SSRF callback from the initial registration responses and unrelated traffic by checking the exact request path and method.
evidence_produced_by_run: The SSRF listener received an HTTP POST request to /ssrf-callback after the second AMF registration with different GUAMI.
The POST body contained a JSON payload with DeregReason and AccessType fields matching the expected UdmUecmDeregistrationData structure.
The first AMF registration returned HTTP 201 with the DeregCallbackUri reflected in the response, confirming the attacker-controlled URL was stored.
The second AMF registration returned HTTP 200, indicating the old registration was replaced and the deregistration notification was triggered.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires mock NRF and UDR services because the UDM depends on these for NF discovery and data storage. In a real deployment, these services would already be running.
The SendOnDeregistrationNotification is called asynchronously in a goroutine, so there may be a brief delay between the second registration response and the SSRF callback arrival.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
